module-1-terminal-map-project.label: Project: Terminal Map
module-1-terminal-map-project.summary: Trace a small folder map for a field-notes project by using ls, cd, and pwd to see where you are and move between folders.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.title: Enter the project folder
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.prompt: You are helping a park survey team trace an existing terminal map. Run ls, move into park-terminal-map, and use pwd to confirm you reached the project folder.
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.hint: This is a cumulative project step. Start from the previous workspace and add only the requested change.

help.concept: Project steps are connected. Focus on where you are, what folders you can see, and where to move next.
help.hint_1: Start with ls so you can see the folder you need to enter.
help.hint_2: After you cd into the project folder, run pwd to confirm your location.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.starterCode: # Use the terminal for this Linux project step.

0.message: List the workspace first with ls.
Do not use sudo for beginner workspace tasks.

park_terminal_map_maps_current_location_.content: Current location checked with pwd and ls

park_terminal_map_handoff_terminal_map_r.content: Terminal map ready for the park survey team

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.title: Find the requests folder
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.prompt: From inside park-terminal-map, list what is there, move into requests, and use pwd to confirm you found the request notes.
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.hint: This is a cumulative project step. Start from the previous workspace and add only the requested change.

help.concept: Once you are inside a folder, ls shows what is available and cd moves you deeper into the map.
help.hint_1: Use ls inside park-terminal-map before choosing where to go next.
help.hint_2: Run pwd after cd requests so you can verify the folder name.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.starterCode: # Use the terminal for this Linux project step.

0.message: Do not use sudo for beginner workspace tasks.

park_terminal_map_maps_current_location_.content: Current location checked with pwd and ls

park_terminal_map_handoff_terminal_map_r.content: Terminal map ready for the park survey team

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.title: Move from requests to maps
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.prompt: From requests, move back to park-terminal-map, enter maps, and use pwd to confirm you reached the navigation notes.
module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.hint: This is a cumulative project step. Start from the previous workspace and add only the requested change.

help.concept: cd .. moves you up one level, which is useful when you need to move from one sibling folder to another.
help.hint_1: Leave requests with cd .. before trying to enter maps.
help.hint_2: Use pwd at the end so you can confirm that maps is your current folder.

module-1-terminal-map-project-terminal-t.starterCode: # Use the terminal for this Linux project step.

0.message: Move back up one level first with cd ..
Do not use sudo for beginner workspace tasks.

park_terminal_map_maps_current_location_.content: Current location checked with pwd and ls

park_terminal_map_handoff_terminal_map_r.content: Terminal map ready for the park survey team

project-synopsis.title: Terminal Map field-notes project synopsis
project-synopsis.bodyMarkdown: You are helping a park operations team trace an existing folder map before a volunteer event. In this project, you will use `ls` to see what is available, `cd` to move between folders, and `pwd` to confirm your location. Each step builds on the previous workspace, so focus on finding the right place instead of creating new files.
